race.name: Gran Fondo Vosges
race.tagline: Longest UCI Gran Fondo qualifier in the forested Vosges mountains with relentless climbs

vitals.location: La Bresse Hohneck ski station, Vosges, France

distance_options.name: Mediofondo
distance_options.qualifier: Women all ages, men 60+ for UCI Worlds

climate.overview: Mid-May mountain spring: 10-18°C (50-64°F) daytime, variable with possible rain, fog, cool temps in forested hills at 900-1350m elevation

terrain.overview: Paved roads through Vosges forests with short steep climbs and longer efforts; no summits over 1000m; ski station start/finish

logistics.explanation: Logistics score 3/5 due to the remote La Bresse Hohneck ski station location requiring travel to the Vosges mountains, with standard expo and bib pickup but no mentions of exceptional organization or accessibility issues.[1][3]

length.explanation: Length scores 5/5 as the Granfondo offers 175km, the longest in the UCI Gran Fondo World Series, alongside 121km Mediofondo and 60km Rando for varied long-distance challenges.[1][3]

technicality.explanation: Technicality at 3/5 reflects numerous short but steep climbs like parts over 10% on Haut du Tôt, balanced by longer non-steep sections such as the 10km Col des Feignes.[1][5]

elevation.explanation: Elevation earns 4/5 with 2894m on the 175km Granfondo and 2300m on the 120km Mediofondo, demanding significant but not extreme climbing across Vosges routes.[1][3]

climate.explanation: Climate scores 3/5 given the mid-May timing in the mountainous Vosges, prone to variable spring weather that can impact riding conditions without guaranteed ideal temperatures.[1]

altitude.explanation: Altitude at 2/5 as climbs peak around 993m at Col des Feignes and finish at 898m La Bresse-Hohneck, offering moderate heights without high-mountain thin air challenges.[1]

adventure.explanation: Adventure rates 4/5 for the scenic Hautes Vosges landscapes, lakes like Lispach, forests, and steep unforgiving climbs creating an immersive natural challenge from La Bresse.[1][4]

prestige.explanation: Prestige is 3/5 as a UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier for championships in its 8th edition, established but not elite-tier in the series.[1][3]

race_quality.explanation: Race quality scores 4/5 for well-structured UCI qualifier routes with precise distances, multiple climbs, and top 25% age-group qualification rewards in a successful annual event.[3][5]

experience.explanation: Experience merits 5/5 with stunning Vosges mountain scenery, lake tours, expo village, and post-race regional attractions like Colmar and Gérardmer enhancing the full event immersion.[1][4]

community.explanation: Community at 4/5 fostered by club and friend involvement encouragement, multiple distances suiting all levels, and shared UCI qualification pursuit in the vibrant Vosges cycling scene.[3]

field_depth.explanation: Field depth scores 3/5 as a regional qualifier drawing committed age-group competitors for UCI Worlds slots, but lacking indications of deep international pro-level fields.[1][5]

value.explanation: Value is 4/5 providing high-quality UCI Granfondo experience with long challenging routes and qualification opportunities at accessible multi-distance pricing for enthusiasts.[3]

expenses.explanation: Expenses score 3/5 due to costs tied to remote ski station venue, potential mountain travel, and accommodations in touristy Vosges without standout affordability mentions.[4]

biased_opinion.overview: Iconic UCI qualifier with longest Granfondo distance in series; forested Vosges climbs deliver suffering without extreme altitude; perfect for age-group qualification hunters seeking Worlds spot. Ski station vibe + pasta party = memorable finish.[1][2][5]

final_verdict.one_liner: T2: Premier UCI Gran Fondo qualifier with exceptional length and scenic suffering in accessible European mountains.

logistics.overview: La Bresse Hohneck ski station (elev. 1100-1350m), 9km above La Bresse town. Nearest airports: Strasbourg (SXB ~150km), Basel-Mulhouse (BSL ~150km). Family-friendly summer bike park area with kids activities.[1]

history.overview: First edition ~2018 (9th edition 2026). UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier since 2021 (6th consecutive year 2026). Grew from regional cyclosportif to international qualifier.[1][2][4]

course_description.character: Starts La Bresse-Hohneck → Lispach lake → **Suffering Zone 1: La Croix des Moinats (891m, early steep)** → **Haut du Tôt (866m, 5.5km sections >10%)** → Col des Ecureuils (678m) → **Col de Haux Jacques (605m, mid-race grind)** → Col du Champdray (733m) → **Suffering Zone 2: Col des Feignes (993m, 10km longest climb)** → 9km finish climb. 3 feed stations + pasta party.[1][2]

guide_variables.suffering_index: High: successive Vosges climbs accumulate fatigue

non_negotiables: Early registration for Worlds qualifier slots

research_metadata.data_quality: High for distances/elevation (UCI+Strava), medium for logistics

youtube_data.search_query: Gran Fondo Vosges gran fondo cycling La Bresse Hohneck ski station

videos.title: Impossible Climb La Bresse 2025 | The Hill Climb of Carnage
videos.description:
Get ready for a breathtaking spectacle from Impossible Climb La Bresse 2025, in the Vosges mountains of France! This video captures the 35th “Montée Impossible”, held on Sunday, 20 July 2025, at the legendary Col de Grosse‑Pierre

Every year, daring riders attempt to conquer an insanely steep hill on powerful, custom built prototype motorcycles. Among the over 50 elite competitors, you’ll spot both French and U.S. champions taking their best shot. It’s also the opening round of the 2025 French Hill Climb Championship, with multiple elimination rounds and a thrilling final showdown

*In this video you’ll see:*
Riders battling through four or five intense heats, with timing and precision determining who reaches the final round .
Spectacular climbs, near-misses, and dramatic wheelspin moments.
The raw power, suspense, and excitement that make this event a crowd favorite for over three decades.

rider_intel.search_text: No race-specific content was extractable from the available transcript. The provided video transcript consists entirely of music lyrics and does not contain any rider commentary, race analysis, or cycling-related information about the Gran Fondo Vosges. Riders looking for intelligence about this 108.7-mile race in the Vosges mountains of France will need to seek additional video sources that contain actual race discussion, course breakdowns, or rider experiences from this event held at La Bresse Hohneck ski station.
